ref: Lot answered, ‚ÄúYou have done us a great favor, sir. You have saved our lives, but please don't make us go to the hills. That's too far away. The city will be destroyed before we can get there, and we will be killed when it happens.
text: N wu fan…õ ma asheŇč b…Ēl…Ē fo kenishi, Ňčkpal man…õ so, fo wora ma kelela ga m m…Ēlga ma Ňčkpa, ama abee na ase w…Ē kuf…Ē ga. Amoso p…Ē…õŇč n…õ n fo ndoŇč kus…Ē ko beeŇč tiŇč n wora ma n…õ n wu.
translated: I did not eat the food prepared for the visitor, and the dog also did not. He gave me a small portion, and when I went to the farm, I saw a goat. Then the chief said he would not let me take the goat unless I paid him.

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, ‚ÄúWhen Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: Kumo be kaman n…õ e kaŇč…õ anya na to be emo n…õ e daŇč juŇčkpar na fan…õ mo da Is…Ē…Ē baŇč tu mo nseŇč bishi mo fan…õ, ‚ÄúWan…õ be esa e la fo? Nn…õ n…õ fee y…Ē? Wan…õ e w…Ē as…Ēb…Ēya damta n…õ b juŇčkpar fo ere kik…õ?‚ÄĚ
translated: When they said he had done it, they were not there when he took the money and ran, as the children of Is…Ē…Ē said to them, ‚ÄúWhere is the way he went? When will he come? Where did he take the money and run?‚ÄĚ

ref: Seir was from the Horite tribe that had lived in Edom before the time of Esau. The clans that had descended from him were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Seyir n…õ e la Horaiebi be esa na be mbiny…õnbi n…õ b daa w…Ē efuli na so nde. Lotan n…õ Shobal n…õ Zibi…Ēn n…õ Ana,
translated: Seyir the Horite, who lived in the land of Seir, and his descendants: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, and Ana.
